title: AppSumo Review 2026: Is It Legit and Are Lifetime Deals Safe?
desc: Is AppSumo legit in 2026? An honest review of the 60-day refund policy, Plus membership, Select protection, the real risks of lifetime deals, and how to buy safely.
keywords: appsumo review, appsumo review 2026, is appsumo legit, is appsumo safe, how does appsumo work, are appsumo lifetime deals worth it, appsumo refund policy, appsumo plus worth it
cta: Want your lead-gen stack picked, built, and run for you?
body:
Quick answer: yes, AppSumo is legit. It has operated since 2010, serves roughly 1.5 million buyers, and its 60-day money-back guarantee is real and self-service, with refunds typically landing back on your card in 3 to 7 business days. The thing that can burn you is not AppSumo itself. It is the lifetime deal model: you are betting the software vendor still exists, and still ships updates, years after collecting a one-time payment. In 2026 that bet deserves more scrutiny than ever, and this review covers exactly how to make it safely.

What AppSumo is and how the deals actually work

AppSumo is the largest marketplace for software lifetime deals (LTDs). Founded by Noah Kagan in 2010 and based in Austin, it has launched more than 2,000 products and built a community of around 1.5 million buyers, who the company calls Sumo-lings. Vendors list their SaaS tools at discounts that usually run 70 to 95 percent off the normal subscription price. You pay once, typically $29 to $99 per license code, and get access for the life of the product instead of a monthly bill.

Three mechanics matter before you buy anything:

  • License tiers. Most deals sell Tier 1, 2, and 3 versions. Higher tiers unlock higher limits (contacts, seats, workspaces). Some deals let you stack multiple codes to upgrade later, many do not, so read the tier table first.
  • Deals rotate. Listings usually run for weeks, not forever. Good deals sell out and may never return, which is also why buying a proven tool beats waiting.
  • Not everything is third-party. AppSumo also sells its own tools, called AppSumo Originals (SendFox for email, TidyCal for scheduling, KingSumo for giveaways). These are the lowest-risk purchases on the site because the marketplace itself maintains them.

The 60-day refund policy is real, and self-service

This is the single strongest reason AppSumo is safe to try. Every standard deal carries a 60-day money-back guarantee, and you do not have to argue with anyone to use it. You go to your account, open Purchases, pick the product, and click Request Refund. Buyers consistently report the money back on their card within 3 to 7 business days, no questions asked.

Two caveats. First, the window is hard: at day 61 it is closed permanently, so treat every purchase as a 60-day trial and actually use the tool in week one, not month three. Second, the guarantee covers the purchase, not the product's future. If a vendor shuts down in year two, your refund window is long gone. That specific risk is what the rest of this review is about.

See today's live AppSumo deals and check any tool you are curious about against the checklist below.

AppSumo Plus and Select: what the $99 actually buys

AppSumo Plus is the $99 per year membership. Per AppSumo's own FAQ, it includes 10 percent off every purchase, a $25 coupon every 90 days, expedited support, extended access to some deals after the public window closes, the private Sauce community, and free Tier 1 access to KingSumo and SendFox while you stay a member.

The math is simple: the quarterly coupons alone are worth up to $100 a year, so if you buy even two or three deals annually, Plus pays for itself. The less obvious benefit is protection. AppSumo Select is the marketplace's curated badge for vetted products, and if a Select tool shuts down within 12 months of your purchase, Plus members get 100 percent of the price back in AppSumo credits. Regular buyers get 50 percent. It is credits rather than cash, and it only covers the first year, but it is meaningfully better than the zero you would get buying the same LTD from a random reseller.

The honest problems nobody puts in the sales page

An honest AppSumo review in 2026 has to include this section, because the risk profile has changed.

  • Vendor survival is the real risk. A lifetime deal is a bet on the vendor's business model, and long-term buyers estimate that somewhere between a third and 40 percent of LTDs eventually shut down, pivot, or quietly stop shipping updates. One-time cash plus indefinite support obligations is a hard business to run, and some vendors never make it work.
  • The model itself is under pressure. In February 2026, founder Noah Kagan publicly acknowledged on X that AppSumo's revenue had fallen about 50 percent over two years. That transparency is to his credit, and the marketplace keeps operating normally, but it confirms the lifetime deal ecosystem is consolidating. Expect fewer, more heavily vetted listings, and apply more scrutiny to unproven vendors, not less.
  • Listings sometimes overpromise. Trustpilot has AppSumo at 3.6 out of 5 from roughly 1,459 reviews as of mid-2026. Read the negative ones and a pattern appears: features advertised on the deal page that turn out to be locked, delayed, or later removed by the vendor. The refund window is your defense, verify the features you care about inside the first 60 days.
  • Support is split in two. AppSumo support handles purchases and refunds and is generally fast, Plus members get priority. Product support comes from the vendor, and its quality varies wildly. After the refund window, if a vendor goes quiet, AppSumo has limited power to help you.
  • Tier limits are forever. Your lifetime license covers the plan you bought, not the product's roadmap. Vendors can and do introduce new features as paid add-ons outside your tier.

How to buy on AppSumo without getting burned

We run a lead generation and cold email agency, we have bought plenty of these deals, and this five-filter checklist is what we actually use:

  • 4 stars or higher on the AppSumo listing, with enough reviews to matter (dozens, not three).
  • The product is at least 12 months old, with a public changelog or roadmap that shows recent activity. A tool that shipped nothing for six months is already fading.
  • The vendor sells real subscriptions too. If the LTD is the vendor's only revenue, you are the exit plan, not the customer.
  • Prefer AppSumo Select listings for the vetting and the shutdown protection described above.
  • Test the core workflow inside 60 days. Import your data, run the job you bought it for, and refund without guilt if it disappoints.

Browse AppSumo Select deals here, they carry the strongest protection the platform offers.

If you want specific picks that already pass these filters, we keep three curated lists updated: the general best AppSumo deals roundup, the niche cold email and lead-gen lifetime deals guide, and a comparison of the lifetime deal marketplaces beyond AppSumo.

Who AppSumo is for, and who should skip it

AppSumo makes sense for bootstrapped founders, freelancers, agencies, and small teams that would otherwise rent five or six mid-tier SaaS subscriptions. Replacing $200 a month of subscriptions with $300 of one-time purchases pays for itself in weeks, and the 60-day guarantee removes most of the downside. It is also a sensible way to test a category (say, a social scheduler or a form builder) before committing to the market leader.

Skip AppSumo if a tool is going to sit at the center of your business. Your CRM, your email sending infrastructure, your client-facing systems: those should run on actively developed subscription software with an SLA and support you can escalate. A dead LTD in your workflow costs far more in migration pain than the subscription you avoided. Buy LTDs for the edges of your stack, not the core, and keep exports of any data you put into them.

Is AppSumo worth it for sellers?

Briefly, since many readers ask: for vendors, AppSumo is a distribution and validation channel, not a profit channel. You give up a steep discount plus a negotiated revenue share on marketplace sales (partners also keep 100 percent of the first $100 from each new buyer they bring themselves), in exchange for exposure to a huge buyer list, fast feedback, and reviews. Vendors with strong onboarding and an upsell path (annual plans, add-ons, higher tiers) tend to win. Vendors hoping LTD cash will fund the whole roadmap tend to become the shutdown statistics above.

Bottom line: is AppSumo worth it in 2026?

Yes, with discipline. AppSumo is a legitimate, 15-year-old marketplace with the most buyer-friendly refund policy in software, and the savings on a good deal are real. The risk lives in the vendors, not the platform, and in 2026 that risk is elevated, so the checklist matters more than it used to: buy proven tools, prefer Select, test hard inside 60 days, and keep mission-critical systems on subscriptions.

Start with one proven deal on AppSumo, put it to work the same week, and let the 60-day window be your safety net.

And if the part you actually want is the outcome (a pipeline full of leads, a cold email system that lands in the inbox, workflows that run themselves), tools are only half of it. Our AI automation agency builds and runs those systems end to end, whatever stack they sit on.
faqs: Yes. AppSumo has operated since 2010, serves about 1.5 million customers, and honors a 60-day money-back guarantee that is fully self-service. Refunds typically arrive back on your card within 3 to 7 business days. The platform itself is trustworthy; the real risk is whether an individual vendor keeps developing its product for years after your one-time purchase.
How does the AppSumo refund policy work?

Every standard deal includes a 60-day money-back guarantee. Go to your AppSumo account, open Purchases, select the product, and click Request Refund. No support ticket or justification is required, and the money is usually back in 3 to 7 business days. The window is strict: after day 60 it closes permanently, so test every tool as soon as you buy it.
Is AppSumo Plus worth $99 a year?

If you buy two or more deals a year, usually yes. Plus includes 10 percent off every purchase, a $25 coupon every 90 days (up to $100 a year), expedited support, free Tier 1 access to KingSumo and SendFox, and stronger protection: if an AppSumo Select tool shuts down within 12 months of purchase, Plus members get 100 percent back in AppSumo credits instead of 50 percent.
What happens if a tool I bought on AppSumo shuts down?

Inside your first 60 days you can simply refund. After that, standard buyers of AppSumo Select deals get 50 percent back in AppSumo credits if the product shuts down within 12 months of purchase, and Plus members get 100 percent in credits. Beyond that first year there is no formal protection, which is why buying proven, actively developed tools matters so much.
Are AppSumo lifetime deals really lifetime?

Lifetime means the lifetime of the product, at the plan tier you bought. As long as the vendor operates, your license stays valid, but long-term buyers estimate a third or more of lifetime deals eventually shut down, pivot, or stall. Filter for tools that are 12 or more months old, rated 4 stars or higher, and still shipping updates, and keep exports of your data.
When are the biggest AppSumo sales?

The two biggest events are Sumo Day, the platform's flagship sale in mid-June, and Black Friday in November, which usually has the deepest discounts and rare returning deals. New deals launch every week year-round, though, and AppSumo Plus members get 10 percent off at any time of year, so a proven deal is worth grabbing whenever it appears.
